old_claim.note: Smartphones provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct Social Media to Smartphones edge remains because the scoped node is a 1990s web-based social network and community-publishing service category; smartphones are later access and scaling channels, not prerequisites.

source_shape.source_locator: Social Network Sites: Definition and Early Years sections define social network sites as web-based services and identify the first recognizable social network site in 1997; the article treats mobile-specific SNSs as variants rather than as the foundation of the category.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors social network sites to web-based services with profiles, visible connections, and traversable friend lists, with the first recognizable example launched in 1997.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This source-backed scope places social media within web services and online community features, so smartphones are downstream client devices and adoption channels rather than an upstream dependency.

ontology_before: The graph modeled Social Media as directly downstream of Smartphones, conflating mobile access devices with the earlier web-service category.
ontology_after: Social Media is scoped to 1990s web-based social network and community-publishing services; mobile devices remain possible downstream access channels outside this node's prerequisite set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: social_media no longer has a direct smartphone prerequisite. Preserved: social_media still depends on the World Wide Web and database infrastructure.
would_reject_if: The Social Media node is narrowed to smartphone-native social apps rather than web-based services.
A source shows that the current 1990s web social-network scope required smartphones to exist.
A separate mobile_social_media_apps node is introduced and intentionally depends on smartphones.
short_reason: Smartphones are a downstream access channel for social media, not a prerequisite for the 1990s web social-media scope.
